summary: binding.gyp command-substitution executes shell at install time
details: The package ships a binding.gyp at the tarball root containing GYP command-expansion syntax (`<!(...)`) in its `sources`/`targets` configuration. npm implicitly invokes `node-gyp rebuild` whenever a `binding.gyp` is present, and node-gyp evaluates `<!(...)` as a shell command during the configure step — so installing this package causes the embedded command to run automatically, with no declared `preinstall`/`install`/`postinstall` script needed and no user opt-in. The package is published as a Backstage GitLab plugin (a pure TypeScript/React frontend integration) and has no legitimate need to build a native addon; no C/C++ source files accompany the binding.gyp, which is consistent with the file existing solely to trigger command execution at install time. Tracing of the file's contents was withheld by the content-safety filter, an additional signal that the embedded command reads as operational malware.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
